message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1, fixes applied vs main). Venv: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
The file /tmp/pass1_confirm.json is a JSON array of 22 pass-1 findings (each: issue, title, file, sev, problem, suggested_fix).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #2 of 2 for finding INDEX 8 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_confirm.json.
Read that item (its issue number, problem, suggested_fix, file). Then VERIFY THE APPLIED FIX on the current branch: read the changed source AND its regression test (git diff vs main, or just read the file + tests), and where useful run a PoC against the venv to confirm the original problem is actually resolved.
Return: appropriate = the fix correctly addresses the root cause in a sound way (not a hack/incomplete patch/over-claim); complete = the fix fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would catch a regression. Set either false if not, and say exactly what is missing. Echo the issue number in 'issue'.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

{
  "issue": "109",
  "title": "A raised run leaves a partial provenance trail with no failure entry \u2014 the abort reason is unrecoverable from the trail",
  "file": "/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py",
  "sev": "MEDIUM",
  "problem": "GOVERNANCE.md \u00a73 and the docstring at lines 102-103 explicitly promise that \"a caller may pass its own trail to run_study so a run that *raises* (a blocked gate, a firewall violation) still leaves an auditable partial trail.\" That partial trail is auditable for what *succeeded*, but it records NOTHING about the failure itself: when any phase raises (IngestionError, FirewallViolation, PreRegistrationError, GateError, or an adapter exception), control leaves through the bare try/finally at lines 269/522 and the finally block only calls tracker.end_run \u2014 it never records a provenance entry for th",
  "suggested_fix": "Wrap the phase machine body in try/except/finally: on any exception, before re-raising, record a terminal entry e.g. trail.record('run_failed', f'{type(exc).__name__} during {visited[-1].name if visited else \"start\"}', error_type=type(exc).__name__, message=str(exc), last_phase=visited[-1].name if visited else None). Keep it inside the chain (so verify_records still passes) and guard the record() "
}

content.text: Finding #109 was addressed in commit 6225498. Let me look at the fix and the relevant source.

content.content:
diff --git a/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py b/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
index af13e2b..3b23f79 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
@@ -29,10 +29,13 @@ versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may u
 from __future__ import annotations
 
 import datetime as _dt
+import uuid
+from contextlib import ExitStack
 from dataclasses import dataclass
 from pathlib import Path
 from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping
 
+from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
 from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
 from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
 from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
@@ -53,7 +56,12 @@ from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
 from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
 from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import TimestampAuthority, require_preregistered
 from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
-from assay_engine.observability.tracing import OtelTracer, Tracer
+from assay_engine.observability.tracing import (
+    OtelTracer,
+    Tracer,
+    bootstrap_tracing,
+    run_trace_context,
+)
 from assay_engine.observability.tracking import ExperimentTracker
 from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision, GateError
 from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, required_phases
@@ -79,6 +87,10 @@ class GateReview:
     summary: str
     payload: Mapping[str, Any]
 
+    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
+        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
+        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
+
 
 GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
 
@@ -266,6 +278,13 @@ def run_study(
         except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
             trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
 
+    # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
+    # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122). Best-effort.
+    bootstrap_tracing()
+    corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
+    ok = False
+    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
+    trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
     try:
         # ---- INGEST ----
         with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
@@ -519,10 +538,28 @@ def run_study(
                 f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
             )
         trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
+        ok = True
+    except Exception as exc:
+        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
+        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
+        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
+        try:
+            trail.record(
+                "run_failed",
+                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
+                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
+                message=str(exc)[:2000],
+                last_phase=last_phase,
+            )
+        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — never mask the original failure
+            pass
+        raise
     finally:
+        trace_ctx.close()
         if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
             try:
-                tracker.end_run(run_id)
+                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
+                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
                 pass

content.content:
95	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
96	
97	
98	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
99	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
100	
101	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
102	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
103	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
104	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
105	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
106	    """
107	    return GateDecision(
108	        approved=True,
109	        gate=review.gate,
110	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
111	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
112	    )
113	
114	
115	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
116	class StudyPlan:
117	    """Everything the runner needs for one run: the static definition + executable methodology.
118	
119	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
120	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
121	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
122	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
123	    definition).
124	    """
125	
126	    definition: StudyDefinition
127	    source: Path
128	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
129	    authority: TimestampAuthority
130	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None
131	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None
132	    confirm_held_out: HeldOutConfirmer | None = None
133	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis] | None = None
134	    confirm_claim: ClaimConfirmer | None = None
135	
136	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
137	        modes = self.definition.modes
138	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
139	            missing = [
140	                n
141	                for n, v in (
142	                    ("split", self.split),
143	                    ("discover", self.discover),
144	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
145	                )
146	                if v is None
147	            ]
148	            if missing:
149	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
150	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
151	            missing = [
152	                n
153	                for n, v in (
154	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
155	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
156	                )
157	                if v is None
158	            ]
159	            if missing:
160	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
161	
162	
163	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
164	class StudyResult:
165	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
166	
167	    study: str
168	    corpus_fingerprint: str
169	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
170	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
171	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None
172	    phases: tuple[Phase, ...]
173	    provenance: tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]
174	    source_version: str | None = None
175	    feature_matrices: tuple[FeatureMatrix, ...] = ()
176	    experiment_run_id: str | None = None
177

179	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
180	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
181	
182	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
183	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
184	
185	    return _log
186	
187	
188	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
189	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
190	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
191	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
192	
193	
194	def run_study(
195	    plan: StudyPlan,
196	    *,
197	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
198	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
199	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
200	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
201	    clock: Clock | None = None,
202	    secret: bytes | None = None,
203	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
204	) -> StudyResult:
205	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
206	
207	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
208	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
209	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
210	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
211	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
212	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
213	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
214	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);

236	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && grep -n "def record\|def verify\|def verify_records\|_chain\|prev_hash\|class ProvenanceTrail" src/assay_engine/contracts/provenance.py 2>/dev/null || grep -rn "class ProvenanceTrail" src/

content.content:
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return tuple(
209	            {
210	                "seq": e.seq,
211	                "kind": e.kind,
212	                "summary": e.summary,
213	                "payload": dict(e.payload),
214	                "timestamp": e.timestamp,
215	                "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
216	                "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
217	            }

content.text: The `record` correctly chains. The fix records `run_failed` as a chained entry, so `verify_records` will still pass since it's appended properly. Now let me verify the regression test exists.

content.text:
Both early and gate-phase failures record a recoverable `run_failed` terminal entry and the chain still verifies. The `last_phase="start"` fallback is also covered in code for the case where nothing was visited.

One note: the fix records `last_phase = visited[-1].name`, which is the most-recently-*entered* phase, not necessarily the one that raised (a firewall could raise inside a phase that was entered, which is correct, but the INGEST failure above raised before `enter()` appended INGEST — yet it shows INGEST). Let me confirm that's accurate and not misleading.

content.content:
286	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
287	    trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
288	    try:
289	        # ---- INGEST ----
290	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
291	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
292	            try:
293	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
294	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
295	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
296	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
297	            if not corpus.units:
298	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")

304	                "ingest",

content.text:
`enter(Phase.INGEST)` is called at line 291 before the parse at line 293, so when ingestion raises, INGEST is correctly recorded as `last_phase`. The `last_phase` accurately reflects the phase that was active when the failure occurred. The `"start"` fallback only triggers if a failure happens before the first `enter()` (e.g., during run_start setup inside the try) — a reasonable sentinel.

The fix is sound and complete. Recording my verdict.

input.reasoning:
The fix correctly addresses the root cause exactly as the finding's suggested_fix prescribed. In src/assay_engine/pipeline.py the phase-machine body is now wrapped in try/except/finally (lines ~288-565): on any exception, before re-raising, it records a terminal 'run_failed' provenance entry via trail.record with error_type=type(exc).__name__, message=str(exc)[:2000], and last_phase=visited[-1].name (or 'start' when nothing visited). This is recorded through the normal ProvenanceTrail.record() path, so it is chained (seq/prev_hash/HMAC) and trail.verify()/verify_records still pass — satisfying the explicit constraint. The record() call is guarded by an inner try/except that swallows any secondary error so the original failure is never masked. Soundness checks: (a) the entry is appended to the caller-owned trail (the one passed into run_study), so it survives the raise and is auditable, matching the GOVERNANCE.md §3 promise; (b) last_phase is accurate because enter(Phase.X) appends to visited before the phase body runs (verified: INGEST is recorded as last_phase on an ingestion failure that raises after enter()); (c) the message is truncated to 2000 chars (reasonable bound, not a defect). Not a hack, not an over-claim. The companion #110 status-marking (end_run status FAILED/FINISHED) is also wired and does not conflict.

Complete: a dedicated regression test exists, tests/test_pipeline.py::test_failed_run_records_failure_entry_and_marks_tracker_failed (lines 331-362). It drives a real abort (a rejecting gate handler raising GateError), passes a caller-owned ProvenanceTrail, and asserts both that a 'run_failed' entry exists and that failed[0].payload['error_type']=='GateError'. The test would fail if the except branch were removed (no run_failed entry) — it genuinely catches a regression. I ran it in the venv: 1 passed. I also ran PoCs confirming (1) with an HMAC-keyed trail the chain still verifies after the failure entry and the terminal entry is run_failed with recoverable error_type/last_phase/message, and (2) an early ingestion failure (missing source) also produces a run_failed entry (last_phase=INGEST) with verify() OK. Both root-cause resolution and regression coverage are satisfied.

Chain mechanics: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/provenance.py ProvenanceTrail.record/_record_locked (lines 116-160, seq+prev_hash+HMAC chaining) and verify() (line 202).
